rationale: gazelle_py offers better pytest/conftest handling than the stock rules_python plugin, which matters for Flask's large suite with shared conftest fixtures and a test_apps tree; it reads pyproject.toml/uv.lock at the root directly.
module_bazel:
module(
    name = "flask",
    version = "0.0.0",
)

bazel_dep(name = "rules_python", version = "2.0.2")
bazel_dep(name = "gazelle", version = "0.50.0")
bazel_dep(name = "gazelle_py", version = "0.50.0")

python = use_extension("@rules_python//python/extensions:python.bzl", "python")
python.toolchain(
    python_version = "3.12",
    is_default = True,
)
use_repo(python, "python_3_12")

pip = use_extension("@rules_python//python/extensions:pip.bzl", "pip")
pip.parse(
    hub_name = "pip",
    python_version = "3.12",
    requirements_lock = "//:requirements.lock",
)
use_repo(pip, "pip")
root_build:
load("@gazelle//:def.bzl", "gazelle", "gazelle_binary")

# gazelle:python_root
# gazelle:exclude examples
# gazelle:exclude docs

gazelle_binary(
    name = "gazelle_bin",
    languages = ["@gazelle_py//py"],
)

gazelle(
    name = "gazelle",
    gazelle = ":gazelle_bin",
)
setup_commands: uv export --format requirements-txt --no-hashes -o requirements.lock || pip-compile -o requirements.lock pyproject.toml
anticipated_risks: gazelle_py BCR version pin compatibility with gazelle 0.50.0
type_check directory not meant to be executed as tests
